workflow_name: Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop
user_message: I have this escalation email from Litware Inc that needs identify the blockers. Renewal conversation is tomorrow.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-04 13:15 EST
Subject: Re: Re: security posture Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about security posture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
Date: 2024-02-20 15:46 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Litware Inc security posture

Just checking in on the security posture proposal. Our VP Operations is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-01 11:06 EST
Subject: security posture - Action Required

Our team encountered some challenges with security posture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF34-H trap (Skips prior-escalation pattern check at this account); produces complete Severity assessment, manager risk brief, customer-facing draft response, stakeholder map delta, fore
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF34-H failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF34-H failure mode (Skips prior-escalation pattern check at this account)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF34-H -- Skips prior-escalation pattern check at this account
